channel.title: NASA JPL
channel.description: NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) — Podcasts — NASA JPL

item.title: NASA’s CADRE: Mini Rovers to Explore the Moon as a Team
item.description: NASA’s CADRE (Cooperative Autonomous Distributed Robotic Exploration) technology demonstration is sending a trio of rovers the size of a carry-on…

item.title: Preserving the Past: Restoring Damaged Photo Negatives
item.description: This video shows how the JPL Archives performs conservation efforts to preserve the content of the photos in our Still Negatives Collection. Digital…

item.title: Perseverance's Mastcam-Z Views Ingenuity's 47th Takeoff
item.description: March 14, 2023 NASA’s Ingenuity Mars Helicopter is seen here at the starting point of its 47th flight on Mars. This video shows the dust initially…

item.title: What's Up - January 2021
item.description: What are some skywatching highlights in January 2021? Mark Earth's closest approach to the Sun for the year, called perihelion, at the start of…

item.title: Perseverance’s Sample Tubes Ready for Mars
item.description: One key activity for NASA's Perseverance Mars rover, which is on its way to the Red Planet, will be to collect samples of Martian rock and…

item.title: Perseverance Arrives at Mars: Feb. 18, 2021
item.description: After nearly 300 million miles (470 million km), NASA’s Perseverance rover completes its journey to Mars on Feb. 18, 2021. But, to reach the surface…

item.title: NISAR: Watching the Earth Move Under Our Feet
item.description: The joint U.S.-Indian NISAR satellite mission will use radar to observe a wide range of Earth processes, from the flow rates of glaciers and ice…

item.title: What's Up - December 2020
item.description: What are some skywatching highlights in December 2020? Catch the year's best meteor shower, the Geminids, in the middle of the month. Then…

item.title: What's Up - November 2020
item.description: What are some skywatching highlights in November 2020? Cool autumn evenings are a great time to look for the Pleiades star cluster. You'll also…

item.title: What's Up - October 2020
item.description: What are some skywatching highlights in October 2020? Not one, but two, full moons; Mars at opposition; and finding the Andromeda galaxy.

item.title: What's Up - September 2020
item.description: What are some skywatching highlights in September 2020? Spot the Moon together with Mars and Venus, along with the flickering star Fomalhaut, which…

item.title: What's Up - August 2020
item.description: What are some skywatching highlights in August 2020? See the Moon posing with various planets throughout the month, plus catch the peak of the…

item.title: Getting Perseverance to the Launch Pad
item.description: In February 2020, NASA’s Perseverance Rover began its long journey to Mars by first traveling across the United States. The rover was built at…

item.title: NASA’s Ingenuity Mars Helicopter: The First Aircraft on Mars
item.description: NASA's Ingenuity Mars Helicopter will make history's first attempt at powered flight on another planet next spring. It is riding with the agency's…

item.title: What's Up - July 2020
item.description: What's Up for July? How about some moons with those giant planets? Mars after midnight. And are the stars of Mars the same as ours?

item.title: What's Up - June 2020
item.description: What are some skywatching highlights you can see in June 2020? Look for the Summer Triangle rising in the east after sundown, keep tabs on the…

item.title: Mars 2020 Perseverance Rover Sample Caching System
item.description: Watch as NASA-JPL engineers test the Sample Caching System on the Perseverance Mars rover. Described as one of the most complex robotic systems ever…

item.title: Working Remotely: How Astronauts Upgraded a Complex Experiment in Space
item.description: NASA's Cold Atom Laboratory underwent a major hardware upgrade aboard the International Space Station in January 2020. The mission team at…

item.title: What's Up - May 2020
item.description: What astronomy highlights can you see in the sky in May 2020? Venus, Sirius and the Milky Way. With so many of us staying home these days,…

item.title: What's Up - April 2020
item.description: What are some astronomy highlights in the sky in April 2020? This month, Venus visits the Pleiades; Mars, Jupiter and Saturn begin their breakup;…

item.title: Curiosity’s 1.8 Billion-Pixel Panorama on Mars
item.description: NASA Curiosity Project Scientist Ashwin Vasavada guides this tour of the rover's view of the Martian surface.

item.title: What's Up - March 2020
item.description: Looking for astronomy highlights for March 2020? This month, early risers enjoy a planetary grouping of Mars, Jupiter and Saturn in the early…

item.title: What's Up - February 2020
item.description: Looking for astronomy highlights for February 2020? This month is the best time of the year to try to view Mercury, soon after sunset; Mars…

item.title: NASA's Spitzer Space Telescope: Unveiling the Universe
item.description: After 16 years of unveiling the infrared universe, NASA's Spitzer Space Telescope has left a singular legacy. As one of NASA’s four Great…

item.title: NASA's New Planet Tracker, NEID
item.description: A new NASA-funded planet-hunting instrument has been installed on the WIYN telescope, on Arizona's Kitt Peak. NEID (pronounced NOO-id) is a…

item.title: What's Up - January 2020
item.description: What are the skywatching highlights of January 2020? The peak of the Quadrantid meteor shower, Mars rises with its "rival" — the red giant…

item.title: What's Up - December 2019
item.description: What can you see in the December sky? Beautiful pairings of Venus, Saturn and Mars with the crescent Moon throughout the month, at sunrise and sunset.

item.title: Neptune Moon Dance (animation)
item.description: See how the odd orbits Neptune's inner moons Naiad and Thalassa enable them to avoid each other, as they race around the planet. Researchers call it…

item.title: What's Up - November 2019
item.description: Highlights of the November sky include how to watch as Mercury transits the Sun on Nov. 11, plus how to observe the regular dimming and brightening…

item.title: NASA InSight's Robotic Arm Helps Out its Mole on Mars
item.description: NASA's InSight lander on Mars is trying to use its robotic arm to get the mission’s heat flow probe, or mole, digging again. InSight team engineer…

item.title: What's Up - October 2019
item.description: What can you see in the October sky? Join the global celebration of International Observe the Moon Night on Oct. 5th, then try to catch the ice…

item.title: What's Up - September 2019
item.description: In this month's sky, look for lovely crescent Moons at the start and end of the month. The September equinox brings the beginning of fall in…

item.title: What's Up - August 2019
item.description: What can you see this month? In the August sky, look for the "shooting stars" of the annual Perseid meteor shower for some stargazing…

item.title: NASA Climbing Robot Scales Cliffs and Looks for Life
item.description: LEMUR belongs to a new generation of robots being built by NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory that can crawl, walk and even climb rock walls.

item.title: What's Up - July 2019
item.description: As NASA marks the 50th anniversary of the Apollo 11 Moon landing, here are five things to know about the Moon that you can share with others: How…

item.title: Landing NASA's Mars 2020 Rover with Terrain Relative Navigation
item.description: The Mars 2020 mission is facing the most challenging landing yet on the Red Planet. It will touch down on Feb. 18, 2021, in Jezero Crater, a…

item.title: NASA Chopper Ready for a Spin on Mars
item.description: Can we fly on Mars? The laws of physics may say it's near impossible, but actually flying a heavier-than-air vehicle on the Red Planet is much…

item.title: What's Up - June 2019
item.description: What's up in the June sky? Jupiter is at its biggest and brightest, Mercury and Mars appear ultra-close and how you can observe the Moon's…

item.title: NASA’s Curiosity Finds Climate Clues on a Martian Mountain
item.description: After spending the better part of a year exploring Mars' Vera Rubin Ridge, NASA's Curiosity Mars rover has moved to a new part of Mount Sharp.…

item.title: What's Up - May 2019
item.description: What's up in the May sky? A meteor shower produced by debris from Halley's Comet, asteroids named after dinosaurs and a "blue…

item.title: NASA's OCO-3: A New View of Carbon
item.description: NASA's OCO-3 mission is ready for launch to the International Space Station. This follow-on to OCO-2 brings new techniques and new technologies to…

item.title: What's Up - April 2019
item.description: What can you see in the April sky? The Moon visits Mars in the evening, and later joins Saturn and Jupiter for a spot of tea. Also, how to find…

item.title: What's Up - March 2019
item.description: What can you see in the March sky? Jupiter and other planets in the morning, a change of seasons and an open star cluster called the Beehive.

item.title: Opportunity: NASA Rover Completes Mars Mission
item.description: Drive along with NASA's Opportunity Mars rover and hear the voices of scientists and engineers behind the mission. Designed to run for 90 days, the…

item.title: What's Up - February 2019
item.description: What can you see in the February sky? Stars and planets with distinct red and blue colors, like Mars and Sirius. February also brings some pretty…

item.title: Mars Report: December 20, 2018
item.description: NASA's InSight has been busy. After landing on the Red Planet, the mission sent home pictures and sound, then placed its first instrument on the…

item.title: Voyager 2 Enters Interstellar Space
item.description: Forty-one years after it launched into space, NASA's Voyager 2 probe has exited our solar bubble and entered the region between stars. Its twin,…

item.title: Sounds of Mars: NASA's InSight Senses Martian Wind
item.description: Listen to Martian wind blow across NASA's InSight lander. The spacecraft's seismometer and air pressure sensor picked up vibrations from 10-15 mph…

item.title: What's Up - December 2018
item.description: December brings the Geminids, a visible comet, and a fond farewell.

item.title: What's Up - November 2018
item.description: November brings planets, an asteroid, a comet and the Leonids

item.title: InSight Landing on Mars
item.description: When NASA's InSight descends to the Red Planet on Nov. 26, 2018, it is guaranteed to be a white-knuckle event.

item.title: What's Up - October 2018
item.description: Celebrate International Observe the Moon Night with your local astronomy club.

item.title: OMG Mission Video
item.description: Join JPL scientist Josh Willis as he and the NASA Oceans Melting Greenland (OMG) team work to understand the role that ocean water plays in melting…

item.title: Dusk for Dawn: NASA Mission to the Asteroid Belt
item.description: NASA's Dawn spacecraft turned science fiction into science fact by using ion propulsion to explore the two largest bodies in the main asteroid belt,…

item.title: What's Up - September 2018
item.description: September nights! Planets bright! Milky Way sights!!

item.title: 15 Years in Space: Spitzer Space Telescope
item.description: Initially scheduled for a 2.5-year primary mission, NASA's Spitzer Space Telescope has gone far beyond its expected lifetime -- and is still going…

item.title: Mars Report: August 20 2018
item.description: What's the latest news from Mars? A global dust storm is starting to settle, but still obscures the Martian surface. Curiosity turns six and drills…

item.title: What's Up - August 2018
item.description: Dark moonless nights for the summer Perseid Meteor Shower!

item.title: Rare Double Asteroid Revealed
item.description: Three of the world's largest radio telescopes team up to show a rare double asteroid. 2017 YE5 is only the fourth binary near-Earth asteroid ever…

item.title: Sounds of Saturn: Hear Radio Emissions of the Planet and Its Moon Enceladus
item.description: New research from the up-close Grand Finale orbits of NASA's Cassini mission shows a surprisingly powerful interaction of plasma waves moving from…

item.title: NASA Mars Report: July 3, 2018
item.description: A dust storm continues to envelop the Red Planet and Curiosity's labs are back in action.

item.title: What's Up - July 2018
item.description: July's night skies feature Mars opposition on the 27th, when Mars, Earth, and the Sun all line up, and Mars' closest approach to Earth…

item.title: Monitoring Plant Health from Space: The ECOSTRESS Mission
item.description: ECOSTRESS is a new NASA Earth science mission to study how effectively plants use water by measuring their temperature from space. ECOsystem…

item.title: What's Up - June 2018
item.description: What's up in the night sky this month? Enjoy a ringside seat for Saturn, plus a night long parade featuring Venus, Jupiter, Mars and Vesta!

item.title: Mars Report: May 24, 2018
item.description: What's the latest news from Mars? NASA's InSight lander and MarCO CubeSats are on their way to the Red Planet, a tiny helicopter will hitch a ride…

item.title: What's Up - May 2018
item.description: The moon and Saturn meet Mars in the morning as NASA's Insight spacecraft launches to the red planet on May 5.

item.title: NEOWISE: Four Years of Asteroid and Comet Data
item.description: NASA's asteroid-hunting NEOWISE survey has observed or detected more than 29,000 asteroids in infrared light.

item.title: Watching the First Interplanetary Launch from America's West Coast
item.description: NASA's InSight will be the first interplanetary launch from America's West Coast. Residents in some of California's coastal communities could get a…

item.title: What's Up - April 2018
item.description: The Moon, Mars and Saturn form a pretty triangle in early April, The Lyrid Meteors are visible in late April, peaking high overhead on the 22nd.

item.title: Mars Report: March 30, 2018
item.description: NASA's InSight arrives at Vandenberg AFB and readies for launch, Opportunity uses its grinder for the first time in 300 sols, and Curiosity…

item.title: NASA Mars InSight
item.description: NASA's next mission to Mars is weeks away from launch. InSight is more than a Mars mission. Its team members hope to unlock the mysteries of the…

item.title: Mars in a Minute: Are There Quakes on Mars?
item.description: Are there earthquakes on Mars? Or rather, "marsquakes?" And what could they teach us about the Red Planet? Find out more in this 60-second video…

item.title: What's Up - March 2018
item.description: At sunset, catch elusive Mercury, bright Venus, the Zodiacal Light, Mars, Saturn, and Jupiter between midnight and dawn.

item.title: What's Up - February 2018
item.description: This month, in honor of Valentine's Day, we look at celestial star pairs and constellation couples.
